title: Blocks, CAPTCHAs, Rate Signals, Identity, and No-Evasion Boundary · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs blocks, captchas, rate signals, identity, and no-evasion boundary from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for block detection and owner-escalation policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Detect denial, challenge, login, consent, interstitial, throttling, suspicious redirects, and semantic block pages from multiple signals.
Treat CAPTCHA and human-verification challenges as stop and owner-coordination signals rather than puzzles to automate around.
Use an honest stable identity and refuse proxy rotation, fingerprint spoofing, account cycling, stealth plugins, or other defense evasion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Record what required effort · Review Block False Positive Review
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Block False Positive Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review site-quality team handles case 6194 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to detect blocks and challenges, stop without evasion, preserve evidence, and coordinate with the target owner; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test block classification against changed fixtures and require human review before deleting data, escalating, or resuming.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Record what required effort · Review Block False Positive Review.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Record what required effort · Review Block False Positive Review, keep Block False Positive Review connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test block classification against changed fixtures and require human review before deleting data, escalating, or resuming.
